plan:
INTEGRATED CONCLUSION: infeasible-under-frame. Both source contracts are individually coherent, but their conjunction on the mandated substrate (Node ≥ 18 native ESM, zero runtime deps, no runtime loader mediation, no dangerous-form scanner, no manual assertion, strictly net-negative symmetric surface) admits no positive construction. I integrated by DESCENT FROM BOTH SOURCES: the constraints contract supplies the frozen twelve-invariant frame and its exhaustion criteria for a valid infeasibility verdict (Section 8: state assumptions, partition the positive-construction classes, show every class violates a frozen invariant, show the only escapes are the forbidden ones); the implementation source supplies exactly such a partition (Lemmas 1–3) plus a non-strawman strongest candidate (SCL). I verified the implementation seat's argument AGAINST the constraints text clause-by-clause rather than selecting one contract over the other; where the constraints text appeared to offer an escape hatch, I tested the hatch and record below why each closes. No blending: the tension is carried forward intact and this verdict is submitted for verifier surfacing.

=== ASSUMPTIONS (per Section 8 requirement) ===
(A0) Units contain author-controlled executable behavior; this is not an assumption but forced by the frame itself: proof case 1 requires a unit "exporting a function," and proof case 6 requires a permitted capability whose in-domain invocations SUCCEED at runtime "according to its modeled semantics." A data-only value model cannot express case 6's success semantics. Therefore some evaluator E must execute unit-authored behavior. (Implementation source Lemma 1; constraints Section 7 cases 1 and 6.)
(A1) Node ≥ 18 native ESM realm semantics: every evaluated module has (a) the dynamic import() expression form accepting a computed string, host-implemented; (b) eval/Function reachable via the intrinsic prototype chain (({}).constructor.constructor) from any object the unit touches; (c) globalThis and the full intrinsic graph. These are host-provided ambient authorities, not import edges; they are pinned ECMA-262 + Node semantics within the audited profile.
(A2) Rice's theorem: "this program, under native JS semantics, never evaluates a loader-capable expression / never causes a loader-capable value to cross a boundary" is a non-trivial semantic property of Turing-complete guest code, hence undecidable. Every decidable sound approximation either rejects by recognizable syntactic/aliasing form (a dangerous-form scanner per the constraints' own definition in Section 1) or admits a residual route (forbidden by "No residual path," Section 5, and by Run 1's postmortem).

=== EXHAUSTIVE PARTITION OF POSITIVE-CONSTRUCTION CLASSES (Lemma 2, verified exhaustive) ===
Any evaluator E for unit behavior is exactly one of:
(E1) the native Node ESM evaluator, main realm, intrinsics and host hooks unmodified;
(E2) an interposed evaluator — vm.SourceTextModule, ShadowRealm, SES lockdown/Compartment, worker with patched globals, membrane, mutated realm intrinsics, delete-globals prelude, frozen-intrinsics runtime, or any mechanism that observes/denies/rewrites the unit's module or generated-code requests or attenuates its ambient intrinsic reachability at runtime;
(E3) a bespoke trusted interpreter over a restricted (non-native-JS) positive language.
Exhaustiveness: an evaluator either leaves realm intrinsics and host hooks unmodified (E1), interposes on them (E2), or does not evaluate JavaScript-with-native-semantics at all (E3). There is no fourth relation an evaluator can bear to the host's ambient authority. This matches four decades of capability-security mechanism classes (static verification of source; runtime interposition/membranes; language restriction with trusted interpretation) — no third sound family is known, and the constraints frame forbids or refuses each.

=== EACH CLASS VIOLATES A FROZEN INVARIANT (Lemma 3, checked against constraints text) ===
• E1 violates LOAD-C-INV-3 and C2 directly: by (A1), an ambient dynamic resolver (import()) and a generated-code route to module authority (Function/eval via intrinsic chain) exist in EVERY natively evaluated module. C2 demands the absence follow from the closed construction and host-interface model, "not from source-form recognition or a runtime denial mechanism." Removing the routes from E1 without interposition requires proving unit code never evaluates them — undecidable by (A2); every decidable sound approximation is either a scanner (forbidden by C2/Section 5 "No recognizer substitution" and by this run's explicit prohibition) or leaves the Run 1 aliased-value residual (forbidden by Section 5 "No residual path"). Manual annotation variants are separately forbidden by LOAD-C-INV-5/C4.
• E2 IS runtime loader mediation by the constraints' own Section 1 definition ("any runtime mechanism that observes, traps, rewrites, approves, denies, resolves, or acquires a unit's module or generated-code request") — expressly forbidden, and Run 1's exact recorded failure. TESTED ESCAPE HATCH #1: the constraints' carve-out "Selection among already established finite targets is not automatically loader mediation" does not rescue a frozen-module-map compartment (the implementation source's recorded relaxation (i)): such a compartment must still TRAP the unit's import()/Function requests to hard-fail them — trapping/denying a generated-code or module request is the definitional act of mediation regardless of whether resolution succeeds, and safety would depend on that runtime denial, which Section 1 forbids ("Safety that depends on such a runtime decision is forbidden"). The carve-out permits selection among pre-established targets; it does not permit the interposition machinery needed to make E2's ambient routes unreachable in the first place.
• E3 fails twice, independently. (a) It violates LOAD-C-INV-10/C7: a new positive language requires a new grammar/schema family, a new parser, a new interpreter semantic, a new capability-invocation check branch, new failure outcomes, and new synchronization obligations — each a mandatory after-side ledger row under C7's categories (schema, parser, validation-branch, failure-outcome, sync-obligation, runtime-machinery, proof-mechanism). The retirable before-side (scanner, safe-export path, recognizer coupling, independent closure derivation) is strictly smaller; the implementation seat's computed partial ledger was 9 added vs 4 retired, and I could not compress the after-side below the before-side under the constraints' one-row-per-independent-distinction rule without asymmetric grouping, which C7 step 4/7 refuses. Δ ≥ 0 ⇒ mandatory refusal (proof case 12 fires against the candidate itself). (b) Under honest classification a trusted interpreter that checks each capability invocation against the closure at call time is an interposed resolver/evaluator standing between unit code and authority — E2 relocated and renamed; the constraints' Section 1 mediation definition ("resolves ... a unit's module or generated-code request"; safety depending on a runtime decision) captures it. TESTED ESCAPE HATCH #2: a purely DECLARATIVE capability model (capabilities interpreted at gate time, never invoked) evades (b) but then cannot satisfy proof case 6's runtime success semantics (A0), collapsing to the data-only contradiction; and it still fails (a).

=== ONLY-ESCAPES DEMONSTRATION (Section 8 requirement) ===
The escapes from this trilemma are exactly the moves the frame forbids: (1) accept a residual static hole (Run 1's aliased-value residual; Section 5 "No residual path"); (2) runtime loader mediation (E2; forbidden and Run 1's failure); (3) dangerous-form scanning or its longer-blacklist variants (Section 5 "No recognizer substitution"); (4) manual safety assertions (LOAD-C-INV-5); (5) a path race / weakened snapshot (LOAD-C-INV-8 forbids — and is anyway not an escape from the evaluator trilemma); (6) non-negative surface accepted qualitatively (LOAD-C-INV-10/proof case 12 forbid). This exhausts Section 8's enumerated escape set. Failure is therefore mechanism-class exhaustion, not failure of one preferred implementation: the strongest candidate (SCL, implementation source Section A) was built in good faith, and its sub-results are individually feasible — LOAD-C-INV-8/C6 race-free byte acquisition via pinned Git object-store reads (see source_snapshot_mechanism) and LOAD-C-INV-1/C1 canonical content-addressed closure are both realizable on Node 18 with zero runtime deps and SHOULD be salvaged by any successor frame. The collapse point is precisely the conjunction {LOAD-C-INV-3/C2 on native ESM} ∧ {no E2} ∧ {no scanner} ∧ {no annotation} ∧ {LOAD-C-INV-10 refuses E3} ∧ {proof cases 1 and 6 force some E}.

=== WHAT THIS INTEGRATION DID NOT DO ===
No runtime mediation was substituted; no residual accepted; no blacklist added; no annotation-based proof; neither source contract silently altered or selected over the other — the constraints frame is applied unweakened, and the implementation seat's impossibility argument was independently re-verified against it, including adversarial testing of both apparent escape hatches (finite-selection carve-out; declarative-capability variant). The recorded frame-level relaxations that would restore feasibility — (i) a gate-time-frozen, resolver-free execution partition with a statically closed module map, or (ii) a capability DSL with a re-baselined C7 ledger counting all retired legacy machinery — are decisions above the integrator's authority and are carried forward, not adopted. The genuine conflict (proof cases 1/6 demand executable capability semantics; C2 + the mediation/scanner/annotation prohibitions + C7 jointly deny every evaluator class that could provide them) survives to verification, as required.
